title: Online lap-time innovation and uncertainty features
summary: Recent F1 degradation work models lap pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets and evaluates it with one-step-ahead rolling-origin validation.
rationale: Recent F1 degradation work models lap pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets and evaluates it with one-step-ahead rolling-origin validation. For this task, the useful signal may be not just expected degradation, but surprise: large positive one-step forecast errors, rising predictive variance, or repeated adverse innovations can mark a driver entering an earlier-than-expected pit window.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Year-Driver sequence, fit a simple online filter on LapTime (s) or LapTime_Delta using only prior laps in that stint; emit per-row features such as predicted next-lap pace, residual z-score, rolling innovation mean, and posterior uncertainty. Feed those features into the existing tabular classifier rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Should add a leakage-safe, adaptive notion of tyre distress and separate true pit pressure from static TyreLife heuristics.
risk: A too-heavy filter can overfit or duplicate existing degradation features; it must be computed strictly forward in time and reset correctly at pit stops.
